description_long: This house features a modern, boxy architectural design with clean lines and a predominantly white exterior. Large floor-to-ceiling windows dominate one side, providing ample natural light and a contemporary aesthetic. The structure is composed of multiple interlocking rectangular volumes, some of which are set back or project outward, creating visual interest and depth. The roof is flat, consistent with modernist styles, and there are several outdoor terrace or patio spaces visible. Window arrangements are asymmetrical, with a mix of small and large panes, and some areas feature accent walls with different textures or colors. The main entryway is understated, likely integrated into one of the recessed sections, and there are no visible decorative columns or overhangs.
description: A modern, flat-roofed house with large windows, white exterior, and interlocking rectangular volumes. Asymmetrical window placement and multiple outdoor terraces add visual interest.
description_view_0: This view shows the side of the house with a mix of small and medium windows, and a recessed section suggesting an entry or patio area.
description_view_1: From this angle, the house's layered volumes and multiple window sizes are visible, with a focus on the rear and side facades.
description_view_2: The front facade is visible here, featuring large floor-to-ceiling windows and a prominent, angular projection.
description_view_3: This view highlights the opposite side, showing a combination of small windows, a green accent panel, and a more solid wall section.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical placement with large picture windows
attribute_entryway_design: Minimalist entryway integrated into a recessed section, no visible porch or decorative elements
